title: Innovation Economics Final
text: For the final project in Innovation Economics we were tasked with writing a paper on a selection of four topics. I selected a topic relating to intellectual property (IP) rights and how they inlfuence creator incentives. The paper analysed the issues of free-riding, public vs private good and the terms and durations of IP righs protections.

title: Business Strategy Final
text: For the final project in business strategy the case was to analyse the acquisition of the rceently bankrupt Swedish battery manufacturer by Lyten based out of California. The assignment was to analyse Lyten’s rapid acquisition fueled international expansion. The paper analysed the scenario through traditional business expanion lenses as well as analysed how Lyten and the topic of energy is becoming more and more important to governments from a national security standpoint.

title: Thesis Writing Collection
text: During my time working on this thesis I was able to find and collect data while researching how social media gambling advertismenet affects youth. My findings indicate how gambling marketing media is marketed towards younger demographics who will be the gamblers of tomorrow. Relying on celebrities and influencers, to foster a false sense of trust, as well native advertisng and a constant merging of the ideas of sport and gambling these companies ads and media seek to normalise gambling with a younger more impressionable audience. Additional studies show that this younger demographic is more prone to excessive problem gambling and gambling more money than they would be comfotroable losing.

title: About Me
text:
Hello! I am Aidan Mistry, a fourth-year student currently studying at Dublin City University. For my first and second year of school, I studied at Brock University in Ontario, Canada. Ranging from my thesis to machine learning algorithms, this portfolio is a collection of works I’ve developed during my time in college. In my free time, I enjoy watching theatre, playing soccer, reading, and brain puzzles.






TipCurrent Focus



I am currently dedicating my time to mastering advanced data visualisation techniques and finalizing my degree thesis!

In the long-term, I hope to pursue a career in consulting or operations. I’ve always found the problems that arise in these business sectors to be very interesting. I’m confident in my ability to think on my feet and critically, and I feel both of these business sectors allow for those skills to shine.

title: Data Visualisation with Python
text: This project demonstrates my ability to embed Python code directly into a Quarto document, generating interactive data visualisations.

title: Data Visualisation with Python
text: This project demonstrates my ability to embed Python code directly into a Quarto document, generating interactive data visualisations.

title: Data Visualisation with Python
text:
Interactive Plot
Below is a Python code chunk that generates an interactive line chart. You can hover over the data points to see the exact values!

import plotly.express as px
import pandas as pd

# Create a sample dataset
df = pd.DataFrame({
    "Day": ["Mon", "Tue", "Wed", "Thu", "Fri"],
    "Productivity Level": [70, 85, 90, 65, 95]
})

# Generate the interactive plot
fig = px.line(
    df, 
    x="Day", 
    y="Productivity Level", 
    title="Weekly Productivity Trend", 
    markers=True
)
fig.show()

title: New Enterprise Devlopment Project
text: For the New Enterprise Development module myself and a group were tasked with brainstorming and creating a unique product then outlining the process of bringing it to market. The model spanned four core papers, a concept paper, feasibility report, marketing and final business plan. Our took the name RideAware and our product was intelligent heads-ups augmented reality cycling glasses. These glasses would project navigationaal cues directly onto the glasses in a riders field of vision to avoid them breaking forcus from the road to chec directions. The module culminated with a live Dragon’s Den style presentation to a group of real investors.

title: Advanced Pyhton Final
text: For the final project in Advanced Python the task was to compare multiple machine learning (ML) algorithms against each other to see how they compare to one another. I selected a dataset outlinig brain tumour data acquired via MRI. The algorithms scanned for who did and did not have a tumour. Utilising confusion matrices and accuracy scores it wass determined which ML algorithm was best suited for a real world medical application as well as potential real world shortcomings.

title: A Study of and Recommendation for Revolut
text: As part of the Business Strategy class myself and a group were tasked with analsing a company. For our selection we chose the neobank Revolut. We identfied a primary issue as being that the average consumer was not comftorable using a fully digital bank for all their banking needs, and viewed Revolut as an acesory card and bank as opposed to a full fledged financial institution. Through the paper we made recommendations and analysed Revolut’s current and potetntial future business paths.
